tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, potassium, and chloride, suggesting impaired renal function and possible mild hyperkalemia. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephropathy and metabolic derangement. Low HbA1c suggests no chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease, metabolic disturbances, and possible urinary tract pathology. Clinical correlation is advised.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
